description_long: This is a partially smoked cigarette with a cylindrical shape, featuring a tan-brown filter at one end and a white paper-wrapped tobacco section extending to the other. The filter is speckled with small white dots, typical of cellulose acetate filters. The opposite end is burnt, with visible grayish-white ash and some blackened, charred tobacco, indicating recent use. The paper wrapping is slightly wrinkled and discolored near the burnt tip, showing signs of heat exposure and combustion. No branding, logos, or text are visible on the exterior surface. The object is small, lightweight, and appears to be made primarily of paper, tobacco, and a fibrous filter.
description: A partially smoked cigarette with a tan filter, white paper body, and a burnt, ashy tip. The surface shows typical discoloration and ash from recent use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cigarette, with the tan filter on the right and the burnt, ashy tip on the left.
description_view_1: End-on view of the filter, displaying its circular shape and tan color with white speckles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length, with the burnt tip now on the right and the filter on the left.
description_view_3: End-on view of the burnt, ashy tip, highlighting the gray-white ash and some blackened tobacco.
attribute_burn_mark: The tip of the cigarette is blackened and discolored, with visible charring and heat damage near the ash.
